data.title: Vivakor granted Nasdaq path to continued listing if $1.00 bid regained by Apr 30, 2026
data.description:
Vivakor received Nasdaq approval to pursue continued listing conditional on regaining a $1.00 minimum bid by April 30, 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • Nasdaq Hearings Panel granted a path to continued listing if Vivakor's closing bid is $1.00+ for 10 consecutive trading days by Apr 30, 2026.
  • Reinstatement to The Nasdaq Capital Market will occur upon confirmation of compliance; company placed on one-year Mandatory Panel Monitor after reinstatement.
  • Panel issued a public reprimand for Vivakor's issuance of shares exceeding 19.99% without required shareholder approval.
  • Press release emphasizes management focus on executing strategy and restoring Nasdaq trading while building value across its energy services platform.

Original SEC Filing: Vivakor, Inc. [ VIVK ] - 8-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: SmartStop Self Storage REIT Launches $300 Million ATM Program With Option for Forward Sales
data.description:
SmartStop Self Storage REIT entered a distribution agreement establishing an at-the-market equity offering program of up to $300 million. The program allows sales through multiple agents and includes the ability to enter forward sale agreements, providing flexibility to issue shares later at locked-in prices. Commissions are up to 2.0% of gross sales. Net proceeds will support general corporate purposes, including acquisitions, development, and debt reduction.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: At-the-market equity distribution and forward sale agreements (up to $300 million)
  • Counterparty: J.P. Morgan; BMO Capital Markets; Evercore; Huntington Securities; KeyBanc Capital Markets; M&T Securities; Raymond James; RBC Capital Markets; Baird; Scotia Capital; Truist Securities; Wells Fargo Securities; and affiliated forward purchasers
  • Signed / Effective: Mar 19 2026 / Mar 19 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Raise equity capital for growth and debt reduction

Original SEC Filing: SmartStop Self Storage REIT, Inc. [ SMA ] - 8-K - Mar. 19, 2026

data.title: ON24 supplements proxy, discloses litigation and updated deal valuation and projections
data.description:
ON24 announced supplemental proxy disclosures, noted litigation challenging its merger with Cvent, and updated financial valuation and projections.

Key Highlights:

  • Two shareholder lawsuits filed in NY state court allege the proxy omitted material information and seek to enjoin or obtain damages related to the merger.
  • Company voluntarily supplemented the definitive proxy to address disclosure issues so stockholders can vote at the March 26, 2026 special meeting.
  • Goldman Sachs’ updated analysis includes cash of ~$169.0M and derives illustrative per-share ranges of $6.30–$8.30 and discounted present values of $3.90–$7.60.
  • Management projections provided: ARR rising from $124M (2025F) to $318M (2035F) and adjusted EBITDA (ex-SBC) growing from $0M (2025F) to $98M (2035F).

Original SEC Filing: ON24 INC. [ ONTF ] - 8-K - Mar. 19, 2026

data.title: Blue Acquisition Amends Blockfusion Merger Pact, Lifts Incentive Pool to 8% and Expands Board
data.description:

The amendment raises Pubco’s post-closing equity incentive plan from 5% to 8% of outstanding shares and increases the Pubco board size from seven to nine directors. All other terms of the original agreement remain unchanged. The changes aim to better align incentives and strengthen post-merger governance.

  • Counterparty: Blockfusion USA; Blockfusion Data Centers

  • Reason: Align incentives and governance for post-closing operations

data.title: Argentine mining exports jump 82% in January to $812 million
data.description:
Argentina's mining exports value surged 82.1% in January this year compared to the same month in 2025, reaching $812 million, the government said on Thursday.

“Of the total, about $707 million corresponds to metal exports and about $96 million to lithium,” presidential spokesman Manuel Adorni said in a statement.

The growth in exports comes as the South American country seeks to boost raw material exports to generate more revenue and avoid the recurring financial crises that have burdened the country in recent years.

data.title: Strawberry Fields REIT 10-K: Revenue $155.0M, EPS $0.60
data.description:
Strawberry Fields REIT reported rental revenues of $155.0 million and basic and diluted income per common share of $0.60 for the period, reflecting year-over-year revenue growth driven by re-tenanting and acquisitions. Net income was $33.306 million, with net income attributable to common stockholders of $7.575 million. The results were supported by improved operating cash flow that aided recent portfolio expansion.

Financial Highlights

  • Rental revenues: $155.0 million, up $37.9 million (32%) year-over-year.
  • Gross profit: $84.3 million (as implied in filing).
  • Total expenses: $70.7 million (as referenced in filing).
  • Net income: $33.306 million, up $6.801 million (26%) year-over-year.
  • Net income attributable to common stockholders: $7.575 million, up $3.480 million (85%).
  • Basic and diluted income per common share: $0.60, up $0.03 (5%).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Growth was driven by re-tenanting activities and multiple acquisitions completed during 2024–2025.
  • Portfolio expansion: The company added 27 facilities across various states in 2024–2025, bringing the portfolio to 143 properties with 15,602 licensed beds.
  • Lease strategy: Re-tenanting of Landmark/Kentucky locations and new master leases with operators such as Tide, Reliant, and regional partners in Kansas and Texas increased recurring rent stability.
  • Operating model: Strawberry Fields REIT continues to use triple‑net leases with third‑party operators, collecting rent while operators manage facility operations.
  • Cash and operations: Operating cash flow improved, with net cash from operations of $90 million used to support acquisitions and working capital needs.

Original SEC Filing: Strawberry Fields REIT, Inc. [ STRW ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: SilverBox 10-K: Net income $5.716M (2025); Cash/Trust investments $213.3M
data.description:
SilverBox reported net income of $5.716 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, driven primarily by interest income that was partially offset by general and administrative expenses and compensation; for the period April 16, 2024–December 31, 2024, SilverBox reported net income of $3.483 million. The company held approximately $213.347 million in cash and trust investments (U.S. Treasury bills), which includes roughly $12.347 million of interest income accumulated in the trust. As a blank check company, SilverBox had no operating revenues prior to a business combination and its financial results reflect trust investment activity and corporate expenses.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income (Year ended Dec 31, 2025): $5.716 million (interest income offset by G&A and compensation)
  • Net Income (Apr 16, 2024–Dec 31, 2024): $3.483 million (interest and fair value changes offset by G&A)
  • Cash / Trust Investments: $213.347 million held in the Trust Account, including approximately $12.347 million of interest income

Business Highlights

  • Business Model: Cayman-incorporated blank check company focused on completing a business combination using IPO and private placement proceeds.
  • Operational Activity: Primary activities to date were organizational work, completion of the IPO on August 19, 2024, and investment of IPO proceeds in the Trust; the company had no operating revenues until completion of a business combination.
  • Capital Deployment & Trust: Approximately $213 million held in trust invested in U.S. Treasury bills intended to be used for a target acquisition and for working capital following a business combination.
  • Management & Conflicts: Management also participates in affiliated SPACs (SBXE and SBXD), which may create potential conflicts when evaluating and allocating acquisition opportunities.

Original SEC Filing: SilverBox Corp IV [ SBXD ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Odysight.ai 2025 10-K: Revenues $3.02M, Net Loss $17.0M
data.description:
Odysight.ai reported fiscal 2025 revenues of $3.02 million and a net loss of $17.0 million, with operating losses widening as investments in R&D, sales and marketing increased. Revenue declined 24% year-over-year, driven by a mix shift toward Industry 4.0 solutions and reduced medical sales; net loss was partly offset by $3.1 million of non-cash stock-based compensation. The company strengthened its cash position with a $23.7 million public offering in February 2025 to fund commercialization and scale-up.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues: $3.015 million, down 24% vs. 2024 (includes $1.2M from PdM/CBM and $1.7M derecognized contract liability).
  • Gross Profit: $0.871 million, down 25% vs. 2024 (costs decreased in line with revenue decline).
  • Operating Loss: $(18.135) million, increased 45% vs. 2024 (driven by higher R&D, G&A and S&M spending).
  • Net Income: $(17.000) million; cash used in operations $13.703 million for 2025 (net loss partly offset by $3.1M non-cash stock compensation).
  • Net Income Per Share: Not reported in the provided items.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenues declined 24% YoY to $3.02M in 2025, driven by reduced medical sales and a strategic shift toward Industry 4.0 solutions.
  • Channel & Market Shift: The company pivoted from medical customers toward predictive maintenance and condition-based monitoring (PdM/CBM) across aerospace, transportation and heavy machinery.
  • Brand & Customers: Secured marquee customers including the Israeli Air Force, Ministry of Defense, NASA, Israel Railways and a global defense contractor, enhancing commercial credibility.
  • Operational Investment: Increased R&D spending by 40% and sales & marketing by 91% to accelerate product development and global commercialization efforts.
  • Liquidity & Growth Execution: Completed a $23.7 million public offering in February 2025 to extend the cash runway for commercialization and scale-up initiatives.

Original SEC Filing: Odysight.ai Inc. [ ODYS ] - 10-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Exyn Technologies Files for Initial Public Offering
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Exyn Technologies filed for an initial public offering.

The artificial-intelligence and robotics company registered for an IPO with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Thursday. It did not say how many shares or warrants it would offer, or at what price.

The company has applied to list its shares on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol "EXYN."

It said it will use the proceeds from the IPO for growth capital, working capital, and general corporate purpose, as well as paying off certain debts.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: ElectroCore CEO Goldberger to Retire
data.description:
By Connor Hart

ElectroCore Chief Executive Officer Dan Goldberger will retire effective April 1.

The bioelectronic-technology company didn't say Thursday who would succeed Goldberger, who will continue advising electroCore as a consultant for the next year.

Joshua Lev, the company's finance chief, will assume the role of interim president, and Michael Fox will join as chief operating officer next month, electroCore said.

The leadership changes came as electroCore posted a net loss of $14 million, or $1.65 a share, last year, compared with a loss of $11.9 million, or $1.59 a share, in 2024.

Net sales climbed 27% to $32 million. ElectroCore guided for revenue growth of about 30% this year.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Live Nation CEO Grilled Over Ticket Fees at Antitrust Trial — WSJ
data.description:
By James Fanelli and Dave Michaels

Live Nation Chief Executive Michael Rapino was grilled Thursday at the concert promoter's monopolization trial, as he sought to defend the company's ticket fees and past boasts about its dominant market share and profitability.

At one point during questioning, Rapino was confronted with a message in which he allegedly told a frustrated booking agent that fees were "too high."

A lawyer for a coalition of more than 20 states, which are suing the company for stifling competition, hammered Rapino for previously bragging that his business was "recession proof" and that he had "built an incredible moat around the castle of Live Nation."

"What is the moat?" the lawyer, Jeffrey Kessler, asked.

Rapino played down the comment, testifying that he meant Live Nation, which owns Ticketmaster, successfully took a fragmented industry and "put the pieces together to make it a global, attractive business for artists."

The lawyer didn't buy the answer, charging that the moat was Live Nation's alleged practice of threatening venues into using Ticketmaster to secure the best concerts. "That's the moat; that's the protection you have," the lawyer said.

The Justice Department settled with Live Nation last week, dropping out of the antitrust case. A bipartisan coalition of states including New York, California and Tennessee have continued to litigate, with a goal of securing a court-ordered breakup of the company. A smaller group of states joined the Justice Department in settling.

The still-suing states say Live Nation and Ticketmaster's 2010 merger formed an entertainment colossus that illegally dominates the market for major concerts, harming artists, fans and venues. Live Nation says it isn't a monopolist and the market is more competitive than the states allege.

During more than five hours of questioning in a New York federal court, Kessler pressed Rapino with past statements he made in depositions or on investor calls. In one exchange, Kessler reminded Rapino that he had crowed during a 2011 call that Live Nation was bigger than all the promoters in the world combined.

Rapino helped build Live Nation from its inception in 2005 into the world's biggest concert promoter through a series of acquisitions. The states allege the company has threatened big venues that don't use Ticketmaster by saying it would withhold concerts from them.

The CEO testified that he had previously said concert tickets had been historically undervalued. He told Kessler that he meant many tickets were being resold on secondary markets for higher prices. "The word you are missing is scalper," Rapino said.

Increased service and venue fees have also led to the rising cost of ticket prices, Kessler said. Rapino admitted that he has heard complaints about the fees from music fans, as well as artists including The Cure frontman Robert Smith. Rapino was also asked about a complaint that he received from a booking agent for the rock group Alabama Shakes.

"In response you wrote, 'Our fees are too high. We can't defend them,'" Kessler said. Rapino said he didn't recall making the comment.

Kessler also quizzed Rapino about the rising cost of parking and beverages at Live Nation-owned venues. The venues raked in more money when they began banning concertgoers from bringing their own lawn chairs to its amphitheaters. Fans instead had to rent chairs from the venue, in some cases paying $15 a pop.

"People were bringing all different sizes of chairs," Rapino told the jury, defending the policy. "It was a safety hazard for sure."

Write to James Fanelli at james.fanelli@wsj.com and Dave Michaels at dave.michaels@wsj.com

data.title: Nvidia to sell 1 million chips to Amazon by end of 2027 in cloud deal
data.description:
By Stephen Nellis

Nvidia will sell 1 million of its graphics processing unit chips, along with a host of the AI giant's other offerings, to Amazon.com's cloud computing unit by 2027, a Nvidia executive told Reuters on Thursday.

Nvidia and Amazon Web Services said this week that AWS had reached a deal to buy its 1 million GPUs but had not disclosed the precise timing of the deal. Ian Buck, vice president of hyperscale and high-performance computing at Nvidia, told Reuters on Thursday that the sales would start this year and extend through 2027.

That is the same time frame through which Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said the company sees an overall sales opportunity of $1 trillion for its Rubin and Blackwell families of chips.

Nvidia and Amazon did not disclose the financial terms of their deal. But Buck told Reuters the transaction contains a broad mix of Nvidia chips beyond the 1 million GPUs, including Nvidia's Spectrum networking chips and the Groq chips that Nvidia released this week after its $17 billion licensing deal with an AI chip startup late last year.

In particular, AWS plans to use a combination of Nvidia's Groq chips, along with six others from Nvidia, for more efficient inference, the name for the process by which AI systems generate answers and carry out tasks on behalf of users.

"Inference is hard. It's wickedly hard," Buck told Reuters. "To be the best at inference, it is not a one chip pony. We actually use all seven chips."

The deal also includes putting Nvidia's Connect X and Spectrum X networking gear in AWS data centers. That move is significant because AWS data centers use custom networking equipment that AWS has spent years perfecting.

"They're still going to do that, of course," Buck said. "But we are collaborating now on deploying Connect X and Spectrum X for those important workloads and biggest customers across AI with AWS."

data.title: Delivra Health Brands Launches Dream Water(R) Kids Sleep Gummies in the United States Market
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - March 19, 2026) - Delivra Health Brands Inc.  (OTCQB: DHBUF) ("Delivra Health" or the "Company"), a consumer packaged goods company uniquely positioned in the health and wellness sector, is pleased to announce that its Dream Water® brand is launching its new Dream Water Kids Sleep Gummies in the US market. Dream Water Kids Sleep Gummies are expected to be available to Company distributors, retailers and e-commerce channels in June 2026.

This product is formulated to support gentle sleep, featuring chamomile and lemon balm, along with 1 mg of melatonin. It is lightly sweetened with honey and cane sugar, contains no artificial colors or flavors, and is both gelatin-free and gluten-free.

"This launch represents another meaningful step forward in our ongoing commitment to innovation and delivering relevant, high-quality products that truly meet consumers' wellness needs," said Gord Davey, President and Chief Executive Officer of Delivra Health. "Our Dream Water Kids Sleep Gummies address the needs of families seeking sleep solutions by providing parents with a trusted, convenient option to support their children's sleep routines. Following multiple successful testing phases, the positive feedback we received caused us to accelerate our planned launch to June 2026."

To view an enhanced version of this graphic, please visit:

In addition to the above, the Company announced that, pursuant to a resolution passed by the Company's Board of Directors on March 19, 2026, it has granted an aggregate of 815,000 incentive stock options (the "Options") under the Company's 10% fixed stock option plan, to certain directors, officers, employees, and consultants of the Company. The Options are exercisable at a price of $0.16 per common share, will have a term of five years from the date of grant, and will vest 1/3 annually from the date of grant. The Options are subject to the terms and conditions prescribed by the TSX Venture Exchange, and applicable securities laws.

About Delivra Health Brands Inc.

Helping people take control of their health with alternative wellness solutions is what energizes the Delivra Health team! The Delivra Health portfolio features innovative brands like Dream Water® and LivRelief™, which deliver relief from common everyday issues like chronic pain, anxiety, and sleeplessness. Delivra Health products have allowed millions of customers to reclaim their mobility, energy, and in turn, quality of life. The websites of the Company's two subsidiaries are Dream Water® and LivReliefTM. For more information, please visit www.delivrahealthbrands.com.

Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements

This news release contains "forward-looking information" and "forward-looking statements" (collectively, "forward-looking statements") within the meaning of the applicable Canadian securities legislation. All statements, other than statements of historical fact, are forward-looking statements and are based on expectations, estimates, and projections as at the date of this news release. Any statement that involves discussions with respect to predictions, expectations, beliefs, plans, projections, objectives, assumptions, future events or performance (often but not always using phrases such as "expects", or "does not expect", "is expected", "anticipates" or "does not anticipate", "plans", "budget", "scheduled", "forecasts", "estimates", "believes" or "intends" or variations of such words and phrases or stating that certain actions, events or results "may" or "could", "would", "might" or "will" be taken to occur or be achieved) are not statements of historical fact and may be forward-looking statements. In this news release, forward-looking statements include, among other things, statements with respect to the launch of Dream Water Kids Sleep Gummies, when the Dream Water Kids Sleep Gummies will be made available, the efficacy of the Dream Water Kids Sleep Gummies, the Company's ability to innovate and deliver relevant, high-quality products to meet consumers' wellness needs, the ability of Dream Water Kids Sleep Gummies to support children's sleep routines, and the Company's products offering relief from chronic pain, anxiety, and sleeplessness.

These forward-looking statements are based on reasonable assumptions and estimates of management of the Company at the time such statements were made. Actual future results may differ materially as forward-looking statements involve known and unknown risks, uncertainties, and other factors which may cause the actual results, performance, or achievements of the Company to materially differ from any future results, performance, or achievements expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements. data.title: Alumis Plans FDA Filing for Plaque Psoriasis Drug After Positive Phase 3 Results
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Alumis plans to seek Food and Drug Administration approval in the later this year for its lead drug candidate after reporting positive late-stage trial results.

The disclosure comes as the biotechnology company on Thursday said it narrowed its loss last year and strengthened its balance sheet with a recent equity raise.

Alumis said its drug, envudeucitinib, met all primary and secondary endpoints in two Phase 3 trials in patients with moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis. About 65% of patients achieved near-complete skin clearance and more than 40% achieved complete clearance at 24 weeks, with a safety profile consistent with earlier studies.

The company said it intends to submit a new-drug application to the FDA in the second half of this year, and that it will present additional data from the trials later this month at a dermatology conference.

Alumis is also studying the drug in systemic lupus erythematosus, with midstage data expected in the third quarter.

Separately, the company said it raised about $345.1 million of gross proceeds from a January stock offering. Including existing cash and investments, Alumis expects to fund operations into the fourth quarter of 2027.

For the year-ended Dec. 31, Alumis reported a net loss of $243.3 million, compared with a loss of $294.2 million a year earlier.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: U.S. GoldMining publishes S-K 1300 technical report and positive PEA for Whistler project
data.description:
U.S. GoldMining released an S-K 1300 technical report summarizing an Initial Assessment and updated mineral resource estimate for the Whistler Gold‑Copper Project, supporting a positive preliminary economic assessment.

Key Highlights:

  • Published S-K 1300 Technical Report Summary and Initial Assessment effective March 2, 2026, with report issued March 19, 2026.
  • Project covers 377 Alaska state claims (~21,732 ha) 170 km NW of Anchorage; no prior major mining on the property.
  • IA projects 40,000 t/d plant, 14.6-year mine life; initial capital cost US$1,278.6M and LOM operating costs US$4,399.8M.
  • Post-tax NPV5% of US$2.04B, post-tax IRR 33.0% and 2.1-year payback using base-case metal prices (gold US$3,200/oz).
  • Updated MRE for Whistler, Raintree West and Island Mountain prepared under SEC S-K 1300; further work (US$68.7M recommended) required for PFS and permitting.

Original SEC Filing: U.S. GoldMining Inc. [ USGO ] - 8-K - Mar. 19, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Skye Bioscience Disclosed Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard
data.description:
On March 17, 2026, the company received a Nasdaq deficiency letter for failing to maintain the $1.00 minimum bid price for 30 consecutive business days under Rule 5450(a)(1). Skye has 180 days, until September 14, 2026, to regain compliance (including the option of a reverse stock split), and its stock continues to trade on the Nasdaq Global Market under SKYE.

Original SEC Filing: Skye Bioscience, Inc. [ SKYE ] - 8-K - Mar. 19, 2026
